Operation 'postSnapToRoads' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original summary mentions POST method implementation detail rather than the core action. Description lacks output details and context about when to use POST vs GET. Improved version focuses on the action (snapping coordinates), clarifies the batch processing capability, explains the use case for POST method, and specifies the response structure for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Snap coordinates to roads (POST for large requests)
data.current_description: Snap up to 200 GPS coordinates to the nearest roads using POST method.
data.summary_suggestion: Snap GPS coordinates to nearest roads
data.description_suggestion: Snaps up to 200 GPS coordinates to the nearest roads using the POST method for large batch requests. Accepts an array of coordinate points and returns matched road segments with snapped coordinates and metadata. Use this operation when processing large coordinate batches that exceed GET request size limits. Returns a SnapToRoadsResponse containing snapped points and their corresponding road information.
data.reasoning: Original summary mentions POST method implementation detail rather than the core action. Description lacks output details and context about when to use POST vs GET. Improved version focuses on the action (snapping coordinates), clarifies the batch processing capability, explains the use case for POST method, and specifies the response structure for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'getDirections' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_context. Description lacks clarity about what is returned (route geometry, instructions, etc.) and doesn't explain when to use GET vs POST. data.current_description: Calculate route directions between up to 50 waypoints. Use option=flexible for truck routing and advanced features.
data.description_suggestion: Calculate route directions between up to 50 waypoints using GET method. Supports standard routing and truck routing via the option=flexible parameter. Returns a route with geometry, distance, duration, and turn-by-turn instructions. Use GET for smaller requests; use POST for requests with more than 50 waypoints.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about what is returned (route geometry, instructions, etc.) and doesn't explain when to use GET vs POST. Added output details and guidance on method selection for better AI agent decision-making.
